data.label: Move (trajectory action, streaming feedback)
data.description: ROS `send_action_goal` to `/xarm6_traj_controller/follow_joint_trajectory` (`control_msgs/action/FollowJointTrajectory`). Commands a single-waypoint trajectory to the proven-reachable joint target [0, -0.3, -0.3, 0, 0.6, 0] (reused from demo 30) on the xArm 6 driven through ros2_control fake hardware. The node declares a Control/Out channel `feedback` (max_fanout 4096): as the controller follows the trajectory it publishes structured `FollowJointTrajectory_Feedback` frames (`{ header, joint_names, desired, actual, error }` — each a nested JointTrajectoryPoint, far richer than the turtle's scalar `{ remaining }`); the ROS backend dedups the DUPLICATED frames rosbridge emits and streams each DISTINCT feedback as an `item` control token (docs/25) into the channel, then a `close` carrying the count. The consumer edge to `fold` carries `join: gather`, so the compiler's per-channel gather barrier parks the gathered collection `{ output: [...] }` on the channel's gathered place, which the downstream `fold` consumer drains off `input.output`. The terminal action RESULT (`error_code`) rides `stdout_tail` ONLY — success is implicit (the backend only succeeds when GoalStatus == 4 SUCCEEDED). The goal is authored as free-form JSON in `config.fields` and passed to rosbridge verbatim (nested array-of-structs, no mapper involvement); `interface_typedefs` carries the flat `FollowJointTrajectory_Result` typedef so the deriver can shape the result port for this non-bundled action. Runs on an `xarm_fleet` runner that satisfies `ros.robot_model == xarm6`; the rosbridge endpoint is runner-local (`EXECUTOR_ROS__WS_URL`).

data.label: Fold Feedback
data.description: Reducer job (docs/25 §2). Reads the move channel's gathered structured-feedback collection (the `{ output: [...] }` envelope merged onto its input token), counts the distinct frames and surfaces the first one verbatim. Replaces the retired in-net `StreamFold` node; proves rich, nested ROS action feedback survives the channel round-trip without depending on its inner field names.
